chief_complaint: A 30-year-old woman presents with recurrent chest pain.
history: The patient has experienced recurrent chest pain over several years. High-sensitive cardiac troponins were persistently elevated, but ECG and imaging findings did not correlate with the symptoms. Extensive investigations were conducted to determine the cause of the elevated troponin levels.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 70 bpm. Physical examination revealed no abnormalities.
labs: ECG: normal. Troponin I: persistently elevated over several years. Other investigations were conducted to rule out other causes of elevated troponin levels.
